title: Decision Filters

content.question: What standing rule could you adopt that would eliminate 10 decisions a week?
sop: Identify one decision type that repeats often (which clients to take, what to say yes to, what to buy).
Write a clear standing rule or filter that answers it in advance.
Rate the Clarity 1-10 of that rule, could you apply it instantly without re-thinking (see calculation, page 4)
Count the Options you're typically weighing when the decision comes up (see calculation, page 4)
Apply the filter the next 5 times the decision appears, no exceptions, and track how much faster it went.
questions: What standing rule could you adopt that would eliminate 10 decisions a week?
What decision do you re-litigate every time it comes up, that you could just answer once, permanently?
If you wrote down your actual decision criteria today, would your recent choices have passed them?
content.equation_example: Rate Clarity 1-10 (how clear your pre-set rule is) and count Options (number of choices you're typically weighing). Clarity 9 divided by Options 3 equals 3.0. Higher means a clear rule cuts through multiple options fast. Lower means either your rule is vague or you're facing too many live options to decide quickly, even with a rule in place.
content.closing_statement: A decision made once beats the same decision daily.

caption:
Principle Nº 163: Decision Filters

Pre-decide what matters.

Ask yourself: What standing rule could you adopt that would eliminate 10 decisions a week?
